what: rajiveutils: Robust Angle Based Joint and Individual Variation Explained
title: rajiveutils: Robust Angle Based Joint and Individual Variation Explained
text: rajiveutils (Robust Angle based Joint Individual Variation Explained) robust alternative aJIVE method estimation joint individual components presence outliers multi-source data. decomposes multi-source data joint, individual residual (noise) contributions. decomposition robust respect outliers types noises present data.

title: rajiveutils: Robust Angle Based Joint and Individual Variation Explained
text: can install released version rajiveutils CRAN : development version GitHub :

title: rajiveutils: Robust Angle Based Joint and Individual Variation Explained
text: basic example shows use rajiveutils simple simulated data:

title: rajiveutils: Robust Angle Based Joint and Individual Variation Explained
text: function returns list class "rajive" containing RaJIVE decomposition, joint component (shared across data sources), individual component (data source specific) residual component data source.
code: library(rajiveutils) ## basic example code n <- 50 pks <- c(100, 80, 50) Y <- ajive.data.sim(K =3, rankJ = 3, rankA = c(7, 6, 4), n = n,                    pks = pks, dist.type = 1)  initial_signal_ranks <-  c(7, 6, 4) data.ajive <- list((Y$sim_data[[1]]), (Y$sim_data[[2]]), (Y$sim_data[[3]])) ajive.results.robust <- Rajive(data.ajive, initial_signal_ranks)

title: rajiveutils: Robust Angle Based Joint and Individual Variation Explained
text: Print concise overview: Summary table ranks: Joint rank: Individual ranks: Shared joint scores (n × joint_rank matrix): Block-specific scores loadings: Full reconstructed matrices (J, , E) block:

title: rajiveutils: Robust Angle Based Joint and Individual Variation Explained
text: Heatmap decomposition:  Proportion variance explained (list): Proportion variance explained (bar chart): Scatter plot scores (e.g. joint component 1 vs 2 block 1):
code: decomposition_heatmaps_robustH(data.ajive, ajive.results.robust) showVarExplained_robust(ajive.results.robust, data.ajive) #> $Joint #> [1] 0.2768646 0.2734908 0.4336092 #>  #> $Indiv #> [1] 0.5873406 0.5611743 0.2963845 #>  #> $Resid #> [1] 0.1357948 0.1653349 0.2700063 plot_variance_explained(ajive.results.robust, data.ajive) plot_scores(ajive.results.robust, k = 1, type = "joint",             comp_x = 1, comp_y = 2)  # Colour points by a grouping variable group_labels <- rep(c("A", "B"), each = n / 2) plot_scores(ajive.results.robust, k = 1, type = "joint",             comp_x = 1, comp_y = 2, group = group_labels)

title: rajiveutils: Robust Angle Based Joint and Individual Variation Explained
text: running RaJIVE decomposition, can test variables data block statistically significantly non-zero joint loadings using jackstraw permutation test: Retrieve significant variables given block component: Visualize jackstraw results (three plot types available):
code: # Run jackstraw test (increase n_null to 50-100 for publication-quality results) js <- jackstraw_rajive(ajive.results.robust, data.ajive,                        alpha = 0.05, n_null = 10,                        correction = "bonferroni")  # Print a concise summary table print(js)  # Get a data frame summary summary(js) get_significant_vars(js, block = 1, component = 1) # P-value histogram plot_jackstraw(js, type = "pvalue_hist", block = 1, component = 1)  # F-statistic vs -log10(p-value) scatter plot plot_jackstraw(js, type = "scatter", block = 1, component = 1)  # Heatmap of -log10(p-value) across all joint components for one block plot_jackstraw(js, type = "loadings_significance", block = 1)

what: Robust Angle based Joint and Individual Variation Explained — Rajive
title: Robust Angle based Joint and Individual Variation Explained — Rajive
text: Computes robust aJIVE decomposition parallel computation.

title: Robust Angle based Joint and Individual Variation Explained — Rajive
code: Rajive(   blocks,   initial_signal_ranks,   full = TRUE,   n_wedin_samples = 1000,   n_rand_dir_samples = 1000,   joint_rank = NA,   num_cores = 1L )

title: Robust Angle based Joint and Individual Variation Explained — Rajive
text: blocks List. list data matrices. initial_signal_ranks Vector. initial signal rank estimates. full Boolean. Whether store full J, , E matrices just SVDs (set FALSE save memory). n_wedin_samples Integer. Number wedin bound samples draw data matrix. n_rand_dir_samples Integer. Number random direction bound samples draw. joint_rank Integer NA. User specified joint_rank. NA estimated data. num_cores Integer. Number cores use parallel computation (block SVD, singular value extraction, Wedin bound resampling, random direction bound sampling). Default 1L (serial). Set value greater 1 enable parallel execution via mclapply registerDoParallel.

title: Robust Angle based Joint and Individual Variation Explained — Rajive
text: object class "rajive": named list containing: block_decomps list matrix (length \(3 \times K\)) per-block       decompositions. block \(k\): individual component index \(3(k-1)+1\),       joint component \(3(k-1)+2\), noise (residual) \(3(k-1)+3\). joint_scores \(n \times r_J\) matrix shared joint score       vectors, \(r_J\) estimated (user-supplied) joint rank. joint_rank Integer. estimated (user-supplied) joint rank. joint_rank_sel list diagnostic information joint rank       selection step (observed singular values, Wedin samples, random direction samples,       thresholds).

title: Robust Angle based Joint and Individual Variation Explained — Rajive
code: # \donttest{ n <- 50 pks <- c(100, 80, 50) Y <- ajive.data.sim(K =3, rankJ = 3, rankA = c(7, 6, 4), n = n,                    pks = pks, dist.type = 1) initial_signal_ranks <-  c(7, 6, 4) data.ajive <- list((Y$sim_data[[1]]), (Y$sim_data[[2]]), (Y$sim_data[[3]])) ajive.results.robust <- Rajive(data.ajive, initial_signal_ranks) # }

title: Extract significant variables from jackstraw results — get_significant_vars
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(42) n   <- 50 pks <- c(100, 80) Y   <- ajive.data.sim(K = 2, rankJ = 2, rankA = c(5, 4), n = n,                       pks = pks, dist.type = 1) data.ajive           <- Y$sim_data initial_signal_ranks <- c(5, 4) ajive_result <- Rajive(data.ajive, initial_signal_ranks) js <- jackstraw_rajive(ajive_result, data.ajive, alpha = 0.05, n_null = 10) get_significant_vars(js, block = 1, component = 1) #>  [1]  1  2  6  8  9 10 11 14 15 16 19 23 24 25 26 27 29 30 31 32 34 37 38 39 41 #> [26] 47 48 50 51 56 59 61 64 65 67 69 70 71 73 75 76 77 78 79 80 81 82 84 85 86 #> [51] 88 89 91 92 94 95 96 97 98 99 # }

what: Jackstraw significance testing for RaJIVE joint loadings — jackstraw_rajive
title: Jackstraw significance testing for RaJIVE joint loadings — jackstraw_rajive
text: Applies permutation-based jackstraw test determine features data block statistically significantly nonzero joint loadings Rajive decomposition.

title: Jackstraw significance testing for RaJIVE joint loadings — jackstraw_rajive
code: jackstraw_rajive(   ajive_output,   blocks,   alpha = 0.05,   n_null = 10,   correction = c("bonferroni", "BH", "none") )

title: Jackstraw significance testing for RaJIVE joint loadings — jackstraw_rajive
text: ajive_output List returned Rajive. blocks List data matrices (list passed Rajive). alpha Numeric scalar; desired significance level.  Default 0.05. n_null Positive integer; number null F-statistics generated per feature per joint component.  Larger values give stable p-values cost computation time.  Default 10; recommended 50–100 publication-quality results. correction Character string controlling multiple-testing correction. One "bonferroni" (default), "BH" (Benjamini–Hochberg FDR), "none".  "bonferroni" divides alpha \(d_k \times \text{joint\_rank}\) block, matching original Python implementation.

title: Jackstraw significance testing for RaJIVE joint loadings — jackstraw_rajive
text: object class "jackstraw_rajive": named list one   element per block (block1, block2, ...).  element   list one element per joint component   (comp1, comp2, ...) containing: f_obs length-\(d_k\) numeric vector observed       F-statistics. f_null \(d_k \times\) n_null matrix null       F-statistics. p_values Empirical p-values (length \(d_k\)). p_adj Multiple-testing adjusted p-values (length       \(d_k\)). significant Named logical vector (length \(d_k\))       indicating significance. significant_vars Integer indices (column names       available) significant features. object also carries attributes alpha, correction,   joint_rank, n_blocks.

title: Jackstraw significance testing for RaJIVE joint loadings — jackstraw_rajive
text: data block \(k\) joint component \(j\), observed F-statistic regression feature ~ joint_score_j + 1 compared null distribution generated permuting randomly sampled feature values, thereby breaking association joint scores. Empirical p-values computed optionally corrected multiple testing.

title: Jackstraw significance testing for RaJIVE joint loadings — jackstraw_rajive
text: Yang X, Hoadley KA, Hannig J, Marron JS (2021). Statistical inference data integration. arXiv:2109.12272. Chung NC, Storey JD (2015). Statistical significance variables driving systematic variation high-dimensional data. Bioinformatics, 31(4):545–554.

title: Jackstraw significance testing for RaJIVE joint loadings — jackstraw_rajive
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(42) n   <- 50 pks <- c(100, 80) Y   <- ajive.data.sim(K = 2, rankJ = 2, rankA = c(5, 4), n = n,                       pks = pks, dist.type = 1) data.ajive           <- Y$sim_data initial_signal_ranks <- c(5, 4) ajive_result <- Rajive(data.ajive, initial_signal_ranks) js <- jackstraw_rajive(ajive_result, data.ajive, alpha = 0.05, n_null = 10) print(js) #> JIVE Jackstraw Significance Test #>   Joint rank: 1   Alpha: 0.05   Correction: bonferroni #>  #>   Block      Component    N features     N significant  #>   ---------------------------------------------------- #>   block1     comp1        100            60             #>   block2     comp1        80             51             summary(js) #>   block component n_features n_significant alpha correction #>  block1     comp1        100            60  0.05 bonferroni #>  block2     comp1         80            51  0.05 bonferroni # }

what: Vectorized OLS F-statistics for simple linear regression — ols_f_stat_matrix
title: Vectorized OLS F-statistics for simple linear regression — ols_f_stat_matrix
text: every row Y_t (feature), compute F-statistic feature ~ x + 1 single matrix operation.  Constant rows (zero variance) return NA.

title: Vectorized OLS F-statistics for simple linear regression — ols_f_stat_matrix
Vectorized OLS F-statistics for simple linear regression — ols_f_stat_matrix
text: Y_t d x n numeric matrix; features rows, observations columns. x length-n numeric vector predictor values (joint scores one component).

title: Vectorized OLS F-statistics for simple linear regression — ols_f_stat_matrix
text: length-d numeric vector F-statistics (NA   constant rows).

title: Plot jackstraw results — plot_jackstraw
text: Produces diagnostic plots jackstraw_rajive object. Three plot types supported (selected via type):

title: Plot jackstraw results — plot_jackstraw
code: plot_jackstraw(   jackstraw_result,   type = c("pvalue_hist", "scatter", "loadings_significance"),   block = 1L,   component = 1L,   label_top = 10L,   ... )

title: Plot jackstraw results — plot_jackstraw
text: jackstraw_result object class "jackstraw_rajive". type Character string; one "pvalue_hist", "scatter", "loadings_significance". block Positive integer; block plot.  Default 1. component Positive integer; joint component plot (used "pvalue_hist" "scatter").  Default 1. label_top Non-negative integer; "scatter", number top-significant features label.  Set 0 suppress labels. Default 10. ... Ignored (reserved future use).

title: Plot jackstraw results — plot_jackstraw
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Plot jackstraw results — plot_jackstraw
text: "pvalue_hist" Histogram empirical p-values single     block / component.  horizontal reference line drawn     effective significance threshold (Bonferroni-adjusted applicable).     Enrichment small p-values indicates real signal. "scatter" Scatter plot observed F-statistic (x-axis)     versus \(-\log_{10}(p\text{-value})\) (y-axis) single block /     component.  Significant features coloured red; non-significant     features grey.  Features optionally labelled column names     present label_top > 0. "loadings_significance" Heatmap     \(-\log_{10}(p\text{-value})\) across joint components     single block.  Significant cells marked asterisk.

title: Plot jackstraw results — plot_jackstraw
text: Yang X, Hoadley KA, Hannig J, Marron JS (2021). Statistical inference data integration. arXiv:2109.12272.

title: Plot jackstraw results — plot_jackstraw
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(42) n   <- 50 pks <- c(100, 80) Y   <- ajive.data.sim(K = 2, rankJ = 2, rankA = c(5, 4), n = n,                       pks = pks, dist.type = 1) data.ajive           <- Y$sim_data initial_signal_ranks <- c(5, 4) ajive_result <- Rajive(data.ajive, initial_signal_ranks) js <- jackstraw_rajive(ajive_result, data.ajive, alpha = 0.05, n_null = 10) plot_jackstraw(js, type = "pvalue_hist", block = 1, component = 1)  plot_jackstraw(js, type = "scatter",     block = 1, component = 1)  plot_jackstraw(js, type = "loadings_significance", block = 1)  # }

what: Scatter plot of block scores — plot_scores
title: Scatter plot of block scores — plot_scores
text: Plots two score components given data block component type (joint individual), making easy visualise latent structure captured RaJIVE decomposition.

title: Scatter plot of block scores — plot_scores
code: plot_scores(   ajive_output,   k,   type = c("joint", "individual"),   comp_x = 1L,   comp_y = 2L,   group = NULL )

title: Scatter plot of block scores — plot_scores
text: ajive_output List returned Rajive. k Positive integer; index data block. type Character string; "joint" "individual". comp_x Positive integer; index component plot x-axis.  Default 1. comp_y Positive integer; index component plot y-axis.  Default 2. group Optional factor vector (length equal number observations) used colour points.  NULL (default) gives uniform colouring.

title: Scatter plot of block scores — plot_scores
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Scatter plot of block scores — plot_scores
code: # \donttest{ n <- 50; pks <- c(60, 40) Y <- ajive.data.sim(K = 2, rankJ = 3, rankA = c(5, 4), n = n,                     pks = pks, dist.type = 1) res <- Rajive(Y$sim_data, c(5, 4)) plot_scores(res, k = 1, type = "joint") #> Error: 'comp_x' and 'comp_y' must each be <= 1 (the number of joint components for block 1). plot_scores(res, k = 2, type = "individual", comp_x = 1, comp_y = 2)  # }

what: rajiveutils: Robust Angle Based Joint and Individual Variation Explained — rajiveutils-package
title: rajiveutils: Robust Angle Based Joint and Individual Variation Explained — rajiveutils-package
text: robust alternative aJIVE (angle based Joint Individual Variation Explained) method (Feng et al 2018: doi:10.1016/j.jmva.2018.03.008 ) estimation joint individual components presence outliers multi-source data. decomposes multi-source data joint, individual residual (noise) contributions. decomposition robust outliers noise data. method illustrated Ponzi et al (2021) arXiv:2101.09110.

title: rajiveutils: Robust Angle Based Joint and Individual Variation Explained — rajiveutils-package
text: Maintainer: Erica Ponzi erica.ponzi@medisin.uio.Authors: Abhik Ghosh abhik.ghosh.stat@gmail.com

title: Proportions of variance explained — showVarExplained_robust
code: # \donttest{ n <- 10 pks <- c(20, 10) Y <- ajive.data.sim(K =2, rankJ = 2, rankA = c(7, 4), n = n,                  pks = pks, dist.type = 1) initial_signal_ranks <-  c(7, 4) data.ajive <- list((Y$sim_data[[1]]), (Y$sim_data[[2]])) ajive.results.robust <- Rajive(data.ajive, initial_signal_ranks) showVarExplained_robust(ajive.results.robust, data.ajive) #> $Joint #> [1] 0.1514145 0.3372620 #>  #> $Indiv #> [1] 0.8121792 0.5820067 #>  #> $Resid #> [1] 0.03640635 0.08073130 #>  # }
